feed.title: Google Cloud: Dataplex
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Dataplex

entry.content: Cloud Composer is now called Managed Service for Apache Airflow. The names for associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) remain unchanged and still reference Composer.
entry.summary: Cloud Composer has been renamed to Managed Service for Apache Airflow. While the service name has changed, associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and IAM references remain the same.
entry.title: Announcement: Managed Service for Apache Airflow is here

entry.content: Dataproc and Google Cloud Serverless for Apache Spark are now unified under the Managed Service for Apache Spark brand. This change consolidates our managed Spark deployment options into a single umbrella brand that includes the full breadth of our Spark capabilities. No existing functionality is being removed as part of this change, and there is no impact to the Dataproc API, metastore, client library, CLI, or IAM names.
entry.summary: Dataproc and Google Cloud Serverless for Apache Spark are now unified under the Managed Service for Apache Spark brand. This consolidation offers a single umbrella for all Spark capabilities without removing any existing functionality or impacting current APIs.
entry.title: Announcement: Unified Spark Service on Google Cloud

entry.content: BigLake is now called Google Cloud Lakehouse. BigLake metastore is now called the Lakehouse runtime catalog. The names for associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) remain unchanged and still reference BigLake.
entry.summary: BigLake has been renamed to Google Cloud Lakehouse, and its metastore is now known as the Lakehouse runtime catalog. While the primary product names have changed, associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and IAM references will continue to use the BigLake name.
entry.title: Announcement: BigLake renamed to Lakehouse

entry.content: Knowledge Catalog discovers links between data assets, helping you understand how they connect and the nature of their relationships. This feature is available in preview. For more information, see View data relationships in Knowledge Catalog.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog now helps you discover links between your data assets and understand their relationships. This feature, available in preview, allows for a deeper understanding of how your data connects.
entry.title: Feature: Discover data relationships

entry.content: Data quality now supports rule reusability. You can now define data quality rules as templates and reuse them across multiple catalog entries to standardize your data quality processes. You can also use a shared library of system rule templates for common data validation scenarios. For more information, see Reuse data quality rules.
entry.summary: Enhance your data quality processes with rule reusability. Define rules as templates and apply them across multiple catalog entries, or leverage a shared library of system rule templates for common validation needs.
entry.title: Feature: Data Quality Rule Reusability

entry.content:
You can now build and run a Knowledge Catalog discovery agent to get more relevant search results for complex natural language queries.

For more information, see Build an agent to discover your data.
entry.summary: You can now build and run a Knowledge Catalog discovery agent. This feature helps you get more relevant search results for complex natural language queries, making your data easier to find.
entry.title: Feature: Improve search with discovery agents

entry.content: To further refine lineage graphs, Knowledge Catalog lineage views include new highlight and filter modes. This feature is available in preview. For more information, see Apply filters and highlighting for a focused view.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog lineage views now offer new highlight and filter modes to help you refine lineage graphs. This feature, available in preview, allows for a more focused view of your data lineage.
entry.title: Feature: New lineage graph views

entry.content:
Data insights for unstructured data transforms dark data or unstructured files in the form of PDFs in Cloud Storage into structured, queryable assets. This feature is now available in preview.

For more information, see About data insights for unstructured data.
entry.summary: Turn your unstructured files in Cloud Storage into structured, queryable assets. Data insights for unstructured data is now available in preview, making it easier to gain value from your dark data.
entry.title: Feature: Unstructured data insights now in preview

entry.content: Dataplex Universal Catalog is now called Knowledge Catalog. The API, client library, CLI, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) names remain unchanged.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog has been renamed to Knowledge Catalog. While the name has changed, the API, client library, CLI, and IAM names will remain the same.
entry.title: Announcement: Dataplex Universal Catalog renamed to Knowledge Catalog

entry.content:
The lightweight profiling mode for data profile scans is available in preview.

The lightweight mode provides low-latency profile scans that return results in seconds, making it ideal for grounding AI agent responses and interactive data exploration. For more information, see Profiling modes.
entry.summary: A new lightweight profiling mode for data profile scans is now available in preview. This mode offers low-latency scans that return results in seconds, perfect for AI grounding and interactive exploration.
entry.title: Feature: Lightweight data profiling mode now in preview

entry.content:
You can now specify a custom execution identity for data quality and data profile scans. By default, scans are executed using the Service Agent. You can now use a custom service account (Bring Your Own Service Account) or End-User Credentials (EUC). Using a custom execution identity lets you enforce the principle of least privilege, use fine-grained BigQuery access controls, and unify scan processing costs directly under BigQuery.

For more information, see Configure execution identity for data quality scans and Configure execution identity for data profile scans.
entry.summary: You can now use custom service accounts or end-user credentials for data quality and data profile scans, providing more control over access and costs.
entry.title: Feature: Custom execution identity for scans

entry.content: Automated cataloging of Looker (Google Cloud core) metadata as well as data lineage ingestion from BigQuery sources are now available in preview. For more information, see the Looker (Google Cloud core) documentation.
entry.summary: Automated metadata cataloging for Looker and data lineage ingestion from BigQuery are now available in preview. This feature enhances data discoverability and understanding within your Looker environment. Explore the capabilities through the Looker documentation.
entry.title: Feature: Data Cataloging and Lineage for Looker

entry.content:
You can now specify a custom execution identity for data quality and data profile scans. By default, scans are executed using the Service Agent. You can now use a custom service account (Bring Your Own Service Account) or End-User Credentials (EUC). Using a custom execution identity lets you enforce the principle of least privilege, use fine-grained BigQuery access controls, and unify scan processing costs directly under BigQuery.

For more information, see Configure execution identity for data quality scans and Configure execution identity for data profile scans.
entry.summary: You can now specify a custom execution identity for data quality and data profile scans, offering enhanced control and flexibility. This allows for the use of custom service accounts or end-user credentials, moving beyond the default service agent. This feature helps enforce least privilege, utilize fine-grained BigQuery access controls, and consolidate scan processing costs within BigQuery.
entry.title: Feature: Custom execution identities for scans

entry.content: You can now save data profile aspects in Dataplex Universal Catalog up to 1 MB in size. For more information, see Quotas and limits.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog now supports saving data profile aspects up to 1 MB. This enhancement allows for more detailed metadata to be stored directly within the catalog for easier access and analysis.
entry.title: Feature: Data profile aspects now savable in Dataplex

entry.content: When you create a data quality rule, you can now optionally include a debug query to run alongside the rule. A debug query is a SQL statement that returns up to 10 scalar values to help diagnose rule failures. This feature is available in preview.
entry.summary: You can now add optional debug queries when creating data quality rules. These SQL queries help diagnose rule failures by returning up to 10 scalar values, aiding in troubleshooting.
entry.title: Feature: Debug queries for data quality rules

entry.content: You can now use metadata change feeds to receive near real-time notifications about metadata changes in Dataplex. Dataplex publishes notifications to a Pub/Sub topic of your choice, letting you build event-driven workflows, sync metadata to external catalogs, or trigger data quality checks. For more information, see About metadata change feeds.
entry.summary: Dataplex now provides metadata change feeds, allowing you to receive near real-time notifications about metadata changes. These notifications are published to a Pub/Sub topic, enabling you to build event-driven workflows, sync metadata to external catalogs, or trigger data quality checks.
entry.title: Feature: Real-time metadata change notifications

entry.content: You can control data lineage ingestion for Dataproc at the organization, folder, or project level. This feature is in Preview. For more information, see Control lineage ingestion.
entry.summary: You can now control data lineage ingestion for Dataproc at the organization, folder, or project level. This feature is in Preview and offers granular control over lineage data. For more details, refer to the documentation on controlling lineage ingestion.
entry.title: Feature: Dataproc lineage ingestion control

entry.content: Some of the metadata that is stored in Dataplex Universal Catalog is changing. This change brings the metadata stored in Dataplex into consistency with metadata from the original source systems such as Vertex AI, Bigtable, Spanner, Pub/Sub, Dataform, and Dataproc Metastore. If you have workloads that depend on such Dataplex metadata, you must adjust them to preserve continuity. For more information about the scope of this change and what you need to do, see Changes to metadata stored in Dataplex Universal Catalog.
entry.summary: Metadata stored in Dataplex Universal Catalog is changing to align with source systems like Vertex AI and Bigtable. Workloads relying on this metadata may need adjustments to ensure continuity. Refer to the documentation for details on the scope and required actions.
entry.title: Breaking: Dataplex Catalog Metadata Update

entry.content:
Natural language search in Dataplex Universal Catalog is generally available (GA).

Natural language search extends keyword search to support natural language queries. It lets you find resources using everyday language, eliminating the need for complex syntax.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog now supports natural language search, allowing users to find resources using everyday language instead of complex syntax.
entry.title: Feature: Natural language search now available in Dataplex

entry.content:
Data products in Dataplex Universal Catalog is now available in preview.

A data product serves as a logical, curated package of data assets designed to solve a specific business problem. It enables faster time to insights and provides trust, context, and self-service access request mechanisms for data consumers. For more information, see About data products.
entry.summary: Data products in Dataplex are now available in preview. Data products are curated packages of data assets that help solve business problems, providing faster insights and self-service access.
entry.title: Feature: Data products in Dataplex now in preview

entry.content:
Previously, data profile scan results were published only to the Google Cloud console. You can now publish the results of a data profile scan as Dataplex Universal Catalog metadata. The latest results are saved to the entry that represents the source table. You can view the results in the Google Cloud console.

If you want to enable catalog publishing for an existing data profile scan, you must edit the scan and re-enable the publishing option.

For more information, see Use data profiling.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now publish data profile scan results as Dataplex Universal Catalog metadata, with the latest results saved to the entry representing the source table. This provides a new way to access and view your data profiling information.
entry.title: Feature: Data Profile Scan Results Now Available as Catalog Metadata

entry.content: Column-level lineage is generally available (GA). The feature provides a granular view of your data by tracking the flow between individual columns within tables. You can perform functions such as root cause analysis, impact analysis, and data source verification for specific columns. Column-level lineage is only supported for BigQuery jobs. For more information about column-level lineage, see Column-level lineage.
entry.summary: Column-level lineage is now generally available for BigQuery jobs. This feature provides a granular view of your data by tracking the flow between individual columns within tables, enabling functions such as root cause analysis, impact analysis, and data source verification for specific columns.
entry.title: Feature: Column-level lineage now available

entry.content: You can now connect your Dataplex Universal Catalog instance to your favorite developer tools, such as the Gemini CLI and other IDEs. This integration enables AI-driven data discovery and asset management directly within your development environment. For more information, see Use Dataplex Universal Catalog with MCP, Gemini, and other agents.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog can now be connected to your preferred developer tools like the Gemini CLI and IDEs, allowing for AI-driven data discovery and management within your development environment.
entry.title: Feature: Dataplex Universal Catalog Now Connects to Developer Tools

entry.content:
Natural language search in Dataplex Universal Catalog is available in preview.

Natural language search extends keyword search to support natural language queries. It lets you find resources using everyday language, eliminating the need for complex syntax.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog now supports natural language search in preview. This feature allows you to find resources using everyday language, moving beyond traditional keyword searches.
entry.title: Feature: Natural language search available in Dataplex Universal Catalog

entry.content:
Previously, data quality scan results were published only to the Google Cloud console. You can now publish the results of a data quality scan as Dataplex Universal Catalog metadata. The latest results are saved to the entry that represents the source table. You can view the results in the Google Cloud console.

If you want to enable catalog publishing for an existing data quality scan, you must edit the scan and re-enable the publishing option.

For more information, see Use auto data quality.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Data quality scan results can now be published as Dataplex Universal Catalog metadata, with the latest results saved to the entry representing the source table. To enable this for existing scans, edit the scan and re-enable the publishing option. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Data quality scan results now available in Dataplex Universal Catalog
